title: Stelis canae
intro: Stelis canae, also known as the "orchid of the forest," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stelis canae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis canae is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Stelis canae is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. The genus Stelis is part of the subtribe Steliinae, which belongs to the tribe Epidendreae. This subtribe is a subgroup within the orchid family, Orchidaceae. Further classification includes placing Stelis canae within the family Orchidaceae and the order Asparagales. This classification places Stelis canae alongside other orchids in the plant kingdom.
